app.name: json2fit
app.tagline: Polar Flow to Garmin Connect migration tool

support.heading: Support json2fit development
support.lead: json2fit is currently a free beta. If the tool helped you move data from Polar Flow to Garmin Connect, you can voluntarily support further development.
support.note: Support is optional. All beta features remain available for free.

beta.bannerText: json2fit is now available free of charge. Help verify Polar Flow export compatibility with Garmin Connect. Files are processed locally in your browser.
beta.interestTitle: Help decide which features should be developed next
beta.interestLead: Select the features that matter most to you. At this stage your choices are saved locally in the browser.
beta.interestPrivacy: These preferences are saved locally in your browser. We do not store workout files or data from your Polar Flow export.

options.otherPlatforms: Other platforms: Suunto, Fitbit, Coros, Strava

landing.title: Move your workouts from Polar Flow to Garmin Connect
landing.lead: Upload your Polar Flow export and generate files for Garmin Connect. Conversion happens directly in your browser, and your data is not sent to a server.
landing.microcopy: Upload ZIP or JSON · download Garmin files · data stays with you
landing.pipelineAria: Polar ZIP to Garmin conversion pipeline
landing.privacy: Your files stay with you. Conversion happens in the browser and we do not send data to a server.
landing.howTitle: From Polar export to Garmin files

steps.title: Upload export
steps.text: Add a Polar Flow ZIP or individual JSON files without sending them to a server.

steps.title: Check files
steps.text: The app checks activities in the browser and shows which files may need attention.

steps.title: Download files
steps.text: Download TCX, FIT or CSV reports and import activities manually in Garmin Connect.

outputFormats.name: TCX
outputFormats.label: safest choice
outputFormats.text: Readable XML for manual import and diagnostics of rejected activities.

outputFormats.name: FIT
outputFormats.label: Garmin format
outputFormats.text: A Garmin activity file prepared directly in the browser.

outputFormats.name: CSV
outputFormats.label: reports
outputFormats.text: Local activity, wellness and diagnostic reports for your archive.

limitations: Garmin Connect may reject a file even after it was checked before download.
FIT is still experimental; if Garmin rejects the file, try downloading TCX.
Sleep and wellness history from Polar Flow does not have a stable public import path into Garmin Connect.

details.title: How json2fit helps move data from Polar Flow to Garmin Connect
details.lead: This page collects details that do not need to crowd the start screen: conversion process, supported data and free beta development preferences.
details.howTitle: From Polar export to manual Garmin import

steps.title: Download Polar Flow export
steps.text: Use the Polar Flow account export and keep the ZIP package on your computer.

steps.title: Upload ZIP locally
steps.text: The app unpacks and classifies files in the browser, without uploading to a server.

steps.title: Download TCX/FIT
steps.text: Export individual activities or a local migration package with reports.

steps.title: Import to Garmin Connect
steps.text: Upload generated files manually in Garmin Connect and review the warning report.

details.reportNote: Missing data is marked as warnings, without fabricating values.
supportedData: GPS track when present in the data
Heart rate, cadence and power without fabricating missing values
Daily activity and sleep as local CSV/JSON/HTML reports
limitations: Garmin Connect may not import sleep and wellness history from Polar Flow.
Not every Polar export includes complete GPS, heart rate, cadence or power.
FIT is local and experimental; TCX remains the stable fallback format.
The app shows warnings instead of fabricating data.

faq.question: Are my files sent to a server?
faq.answer: No. Import, classification, preview and export run locally in the browser.

faq.question: Do I have to use FIT?
faq.answer: No. TCX is the primary export format, and FIT is marked as experimental.

faq.question: Will the app move sleep to Garmin Connect?
faq.answer: It does not promise that. Sleep and wellness data is exported as local archive reports.

help.title: How to move data from Polar Flow to Garmin Connect?
help.leadStart: This help walks through downloading the export from your Polar account, local import in the app, choosing
help.leadEnd: and manual import to Garmin Connect. Garmin may reject a file if critical data is missing.

download-polar-export.title: How do I download the export from my Polar account?
download-polar-export.summary: Download the full data archive from your Polar account, not from the Polar Flow app, and keep the original ZIP as a backup.
steps: Open the Polar account export page in your browser.
Sign in with the same Polar account you use with Polar Flow.
Order the export and wait until Polar prepares the package. It can take a while.
Download the ZIP file to your computer and do not unpack it if you want the simplest import path.
notes: The export may contain workouts, daily activity, sleep, account data and device metadata.
JSON files for conversion come from the Polar account export: https://account.polar.com/#export
Account and profile files are detected as sensitive data and are not included in sport export.

import-files.title: How do I upload files to the converter?
import-files.summary: The safest path is to upload the full ZIP downloaded from your Polar account. The app unpacks it locally and classifies JSON files.
steps: Drag the ZIP onto the import area or use the Choose ZIP button.
You can also choose individual JSON files or a folder with JSON files.
Wait for scanning and classification to finish.
Check the classification report, warnings and Garmin-ready cards before export.
notes: Data is processed in browser memory and is not sent to a server.
A damaged ZIP, invalid JSON or file without workout data will be shown as an issue to review.

tcx-vs-fit.title: How do I choose TCX or FIT?
tcx-vs-fit.summary: TCX is readable XML and a good fallback. FIT is smaller and closer to Garmin, but local export is experimental.
steps: Choose TCX if you want a stable text file that is easy to diagnose.
Choose FIT if you want Garmin's binary format and local validation shows no errors.
If you migrate many activities, download the TCX + FIT package and start importing FIT.
If Garmin rejects FIT, try TCX and check the Garmin-ready report.
notes: The app does not add fake trackpoints, GPS or heart-rate data.
Garmin Connect may reject a file even if the local structure validation passes.

import-to-garmin.title: How do I import files to Garmin Connect?
import-to-garmin.summary: Import to Garmin Connect is manual: download the file from the app, then add it in Garmin Connect.
steps: Download an individual TCX/FIT file or a ZIP package with activities from the app.
If you downloaded a ZIP, unpack it locally on your computer.
In Garmin Connect, choose manual activity import.
Add FIT or TCX files in batches and check the import result.
Compare rejected activities with the Garmin-ready report.
notes: Garmin Connect may change the sport type or reject an activity with missing data.
Keep the original account export so you can rerun conversion after parser fixes.

troubleshooting.title: What should I do if Garmin rejects a file?
troubleshooting.summary: First check whether the activity has a start, duration, trackpoints and reasonable timestamps.
steps: Open the Garmin-ready validation section and find the rejected activity.
Check critical errors, warnings, trackpoint count and GPS/HR availability.
If FIT import failed, try TCX for the same activity.
If critical data is missing, leave the activity out of sport import and keep the report for analysis.
notes: Missing GPS usually does not have to block import, but missing trackpoints is critical.
The app shows warnings instead of filling missing data with artificial values.

sleep-and-wellness.title: What about sleep and wellness data?
sleep-and-wellness.summary: Sleep, daily activity and wellness data is exported as local reports, not as a promise of Garmin Connect import.
steps: Upload the ZIP from your Polar account and wait for wellness/sleep analysis.
Check counters for activity days, sleep nights, Nightly Recharge and daily heart rate.
Download Wellness ZIP if you want a local CSV, JSON or HTML archive.
Do not import these reports as sport activities in Garmin Connect.
notes: Garmin Connect does not provide a stable public import for Polar Flow sleep history.
Missing values remain empty or go to warnings; the app does not create fake zeros.

privacy.title: Privacy and local processing
privacy.summary: Everything runs directly in the browser. User files are not sent to a server.
steps: Upload data locally in the browser.
Conversion, file checks, map preview and reports run on your device.
Download generated files to your computer.
Use Clear app data if you want to remove current session data and app storage.
notes: Offline mode stores only files required to run the app and local help instructions.
Polar account ZIP, workout JSON, GPS data, TCX/FIT and result reports are not automatically stored for offline mode.

faq.question: Are my files sent to a server?
faq.answer: No. Conversion happens directly in your browser and files are not sent to a server.

faq.question: Will Garmin Connect accept every file?
faq.answer: No. Garmin Connect may reject a file, especially when start time, trackpoints or valid timestamps are missing.

faq.question: Should I choose TCX or FIT?
faq.answer: Start with FIT if local validation is clean. Keep TCX as a readable diagnostic fallback.

faq.question: Will sleep and wellness move to Garmin Connect?
faq.answer: There is no stable public import path for Polar Flow sleep history into Garmin Connect. Treat wellness reports as a local archive.

terms.TCX: Text XML format used by Garmin Training Center. Good for diagnostics and as a stable import fallback.
terms.FIT: Garmin binary activity format. It is small and rich, but Garmin Connect may still reject the file.
terms.GPX: GPS track format. In this app it is only a helper concept, not the main export format.
terms.trackpoint: A single activity timeline point, usually with time and optional GPS, heart rate, cadence or power.
terms.GPS: Route coordinates. Missing GPS does not always block import, but affects the map and activity quality.
terms.HR: Heart rate recorded in the activity. Missing HR does not block export, but Garmin will not show a heart-rate chart.
terms.cadence: Cadence, meaning step rhythm or revolutions. It is exported only when present in Polar data.
terms.power: Power in watts. The app exports it only when present in source data.

termSummaries.GPX: GPS track format used as a helper term when discussing routes.

converter.uploadTitle: Upload a ZIP file from Polar Flow export
converter.uploadLead: Drop the full Polar export ZIP or choose individual JSON files. Everything is processed locally in your browser.
converter.supportedFiles: Supported: Polar Flow ZIP, training-session JSON and selected activity/wellness JSON files.
converter.classificationLead: The app separates workouts, daily activity, sleep/wellness data and files that need review.
converter.classificationEmpty: Upload a ZIP or JSON files to see classification results.
converter.garminReadyLead: Check which activities can be exported to TCX/FIT and which ones need attention before Garmin import.
converter.previewLead: Check stats, local GPS track and charts before TCX/FIT export.
converter.previewPrivacyNote: Full preview loads normalized trackpoints locally in the browser. Data is not saved to storage or sent to a server.
converter.noNormalizedPreview: No normalized activity available for preview.
converter.previewEmpty: Select an activity to preview it.
converter.bulkLead: Download a local migration package with selected formats, reports and optional wellness data.
converter.wellnessLead: Daily activity, sleep and wellness data are available as local reports, not as sport activities for Garmin Connect.
converter.wellnessEmpty: No sleep or wellness data detected yet.
converter.wellnessCounterNote: Counters show records/days from Polar files, not file count. Date range:
converter.postExportDonation: If json2fit saved you time, you can voluntarily support development with a small coffee.
converter.postExportSupport: Support does not unlock extra features. It only helps keep the free beta moving.
converter.postExportSupportAria: Buy a coffee after a successful export
converter.diagnosticsLead: Use this section when Garmin Connect rejects a file and you need a technical report.
converter.diagnosticNoActivity: No activity detected for Garmin Connect diagnostics yet.
converter.showFullFilename: Show or hide full file name: {{filename}}
converter.privacyLead: Conversion happens directly in your browser. The app does not send the Polar Flow export or generated files to a server.
privacyBullets: Offline mode stores only files required to run the app and local help instructions.
Polar Flow ZIP, workout JSON, TCX/FIT, GPS data and result reports are not automatically saved for offline mode.
The app does not send data to a server and does not use external analytics services.
Workout data stays in memory for the current session until you replace it or clear the app.

runtime.noWorkerSupport: This browser does not support safe background file conversion.
runtime.workerEnded: The conversion worker stopped before sending a response.
runtime.workerMessageError: Could not receive the response from the conversion worker.
runtime.workerTimeout: The conversion worker did not respond for {{seconds}} s during {{action}}. Check in Network whether assets/pyodide and assets/python/converter are loaded from the correct app directory.
runtime.postMessageTooLarge: Could not pass data to the worker ({{action}}). The data package is too large for browser memory. Try a smaller export or split the import into several files.

workerMessages.accountProfileWarning: The file may contain profile data and is not analyzed as a workout.

workerMessages.noLapSession: Could not calculate lap/session for Garmin.

workerMessages.missingSleepDate: Missing sleep date; record skipped in CSV.

workerMessages.fitMissingPoolLength: Missing pool length for FIT lapSwimming. Activity saved as swimming/generic.
workerMessages.fitSkippedInvalidTime: Skipped a trackpoint without a valid time.

workerMessages.fitPoolLengthWriteFailed: Could not write pool length to FIT despite swimmingPhases being present.

workerMessages.fitInvalidHeader: Generated file does not have a valid FIT header.
workerMessages.fitInvalidCrc: Generated FIT file failed the CRC integrity check.

workerMessages.fitUnsupportedSport: Unsupported Polar sport for FIT: {{sport}}. Generic was used.

workerMessages.fitNormalizeFailed: Could not normalize activity for FIT.

workerMessages.pyodideTimeout: Pyodide did not finish initialization within {{seconds}} s. Check whether hosting serves assets/pyodide/pyodide.asm.wasm as application/wasm.
workerMessages.pythonModuleLoadFailed: Could not load Python module {{filename}} from {{url}} ({{status}}).
workerMessages.htmlInsteadPythonModule: Hosting returned HTML instead of Python module {{filename}}: {{url}}
workerMessages.wasmMimeInvalid: Hosting serves {{filename}} as "{{received}}", but Pyodide requires "{{required}}". For Apache add: AddType application/wasm .wasm, then clear the old service worker/browser cache.
workerMessages.assetTruncated: File {{filename}} from {{url}} looks truncated ({{bytes}} B). Deploy the full assets/pyodide directory again.
workerMessages.pyodideLoadFailed: Could not load Pyodide from {{moduleUrl}} or {{fallbackUrl}}: {{error}}; fallback: {{fallbackError}}
workerMessages.pyodideNoLoadFunction: Pyodide file does not expose loadPyodide: {{moduleUrl}}

fileIssues.zipWithOtherFiles: Upload the ZIP file individually, without additional files.
fileIssues.readFailed: Could not read the file in the browser.
fileIssues.zipUnpackFailed: Could not unpack ZIP. The file may be damaged, encrypted or unsupported.
fileIssues.utf8Failed: Could not read the file as UTF-8.
fileIssues.zipJsonUnreadable: ZIP contains JSON files, but none could be read as UTF-8.
fileIssues.zipNoJson: ZIP does not contain JSON files.

route.title: GPS track
route.lead: Local GPS track, without map tiles and without sending coordinates.

actions.daily_activity: CSV export in the next step
actions.sleep_or_wellness: CSV/HTML export in the next step

missing-start-time.title: Missing start time
missing-start-time.detail: Garmin requires an unambiguous activity start time.
missing-start-time.suggestion: Check whether the source JSON contains the workout start time. Without it, the app should not invent a date.

missing-fit-session-lap.title: Missing session/lap
missing-fit-session-lap.detail: FIT and TCX need session and lap summaries so Garmin can build an activity.
missing-fit-session-lap.suggestion: Try TCX if the problem affects FIT. If start time or duration is missing, keep the report for parser analysis.

missing-tcx-activity-id.title: Missing Activity/Id in TCX
missing-tcx-activity-id.detail: Garmin Connect usually expects TCX Activity/Id to be a valid ISO date.
missing-tcx-activity-id.suggestion: Generate the file again after checking start time. If the error returns, use the diagnostic report as a regression.

missing-trackpoints.title: Missing trackpoints
missing-trackpoints.detail: A file without time records has a very low chance of Garmin Connect import.
missing-trackpoints.suggestion: Do not import this as a sport activity until the parser finds the workout timeline in the source JSON.

invalid-timestamps.title: Invalid timestamps
invalid-timestamps.detail: Garmin may reject an activity when points have missing, invalid or backward-moving times.
invalid-timestamps.suggestion: Try the alternate format. If both formats are blocked, an anonymized source sample is needed to improve the parser.

duplicate-timestamps.title: Duplicate timestamps
duplicate-timestamps.detail: Repeated trackpoint times can break import or shorten the activity.
duplicate-timestamps.suggestion: The FIT exporter skips duplicates, but if Garmin still rejects the file, try TCX and include the diagnostic report.

invalid-gps.title: GPS coordinates out of range
invalid-gps.detail: Latitude must be within -90..90, and longitude within -180..180.
invalid-gps.suggestion: Export should skip invalid GPS points instead of repairing them. Download a report without the full route or with rounded GPS.

invalid-time-format.title: Invalid time format
invalid-time-format.detail: Garmin expects dates that can be read as ISO 8601.
invalid-time-format.suggestion: Generate the file again after fixing time mapping in the parser.

fit-crc-structure.title: FIT has invalid structure or CRC
fit-crc-structure.detail: The local FIT decoder found a problem with the binary file structure.
fit-crc-structure.suggestion: Use TCX for this activity and keep the report. FIT remains an experimental local export.

sport-mapping.title: Uncertain sport mapping
sport-mapping.detail: Garmin Connect may import the activity as a generic sport or change the subsport.
sport-mapping.suggestion: After import, check the activity type in Garmin Connect. TCX has a smaller sport set than FIT.

numeric-range.title: Numeric values out of range
numeric-range.detail: Some numeric fields are negative, too large or unsupported by FIT/TCX.
numeric-range.suggestion: The exporter should skip optional out-of-range fields and keep critical errors visible in the report.

missing-gps.title: Missing GPS
missing-gps.detail: Missing GPS usually does not block import, but the activity will not have a map.
missing-gps.suggestion: Import as a time-based activity or try the other format if Garmin shows an incomplete import.

mime-hosting.title: Hosting MIME affects the app, not the output file
mime-hosting.detail: Wrong MIME for WASM can prevent the app from starting, but it is not why a finished FIT/TCX is rejected.
mime-hosting.suggestion: If the app works and downloads a file, diagnose activity structure and FIT/TCX validation.

garmin-business-rules.title: Local validation passed
garmin-business-rules.detail: The file passes local checks, but Garmin Connect may have additional business rules.
garmin-business-rules.suggestion: Try the second TCX/FIT format and compare import with the report statistics.

reports.readmeNoElements: No export elements selected. Go back to the app and select at least one package option.
reports.notAvailable: No {{format}} files were generated. Check reports/skipped-files.csv and reports/warnings.csv.
reports.garminImportStep1: 1. Open Garmin Connect in a browser.
reports.garminImportStep2: 2. Go to Import Data / Upload activities.
reports.garminImportStep3: 3. Upload files from activities/tcx or activities/fit.
reports.garminImportStep4: 4. If Garmin rejects one file, check reports/warnings.csv and reports/skipped-files.csv.
reports.limitationFit: - FIT export is experimental local and may still be rejected by Garmin Connect.
reports.limitationMissingData: - Missing source data is reported as warnings instead of being fabricated.
reports.limitationWellness: - Wellness/sleep reports are local archive reports; Garmin Connect does not provide a stable public import for Polar Flow sleep history.
reports.privacyText: All files were prepared directly in the browser. Training data is not uploaded to a server.

wellness.title: Polar wellness report
wellness.note: Report generated locally in the browser. Garmin Connect does not provide a stable public import for Polar Flow sleep history.
wellness.noMissingData: No significant missing data in generated records.
